mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market slack signals imminent corporate earnings deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A declining industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when regime is misclassified.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent signals extreme monetary accommodation that lowers borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent (t-stat < 1.5 over 10-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market deterioration and weakening economic activity, which suppresses corporate earnings and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC > -0.01) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 95.0 on its 2017=100 index, it signals robust manufacturing output and economic momentum that boosts market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns show no positive bias when INDPRO crosses above 95.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample tests.
